title: Decoder GPT-2 prompt-stress quality gate
hypothesis: The 24-prompt GPT-2 checkpoint gate found bf16/PWL exact-safe, while q8 reciprocal already showed one prompt-sensitive miss. A broader GPT-2 prompt-stress screen should test whether bf16/PWL remains stable across factual, arithmetic, sequence, code, dialogue, quote, narrative, long-context, ambiguous, and symbolic prompt regimes before moving to QAT or larger-array claims.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does the current bf16/PWL path remain exact-safe across a broader GPT-2 small next-token prompt distribution?
include: runtime materialization of gpt2 into the decoder ONNX contract
96-prompt stress set spanning factual, arithmetic, sequence, code, dialogue, quote, narrative, long-context, ambiguous, and symbolic categories
exact reference and approximated candidate next-token traces
q12 exact-normalization and q8 reciprocal controls
bf16/PWL baseline and bf16/PWL logit tie-break rows
exclude: committing large ONNX or tokenizer artifacts
training, fine tuning, or quantization-aware training
claiming robustness for larger production LLM families

expected_benefit: tests whether the GPT-2 exact-safe result survives broader prompt/input distributions
keeps the next frontier step on the existing decoder quality harness
exposes miss buckets before spending evaluator time on QAT, GPT-2-scale prompt expansion, or larger hardware arrays
risks: GPT-2 small is still much smaller than current production LLMs
the prompt set is a next-token stress screen, not a full application benchmark
export requires torch, transformers, and onnx in the evaluator Python environment
a failure may reflect distribution sensitivity rather than hardware infeasibility

required_evaluations.objective: Check whether bf16/PWL remains exact-safe on a broader GPT-2 small prompt-stress workload before moving to QAT or larger-array claims.
